tagline: Film room intelligence at machine speed.

round_of_64.reasoning: Houston's elite defensive pressure overwhelms Longwood in every matchup dimension.
Texas A&M's length and defensive versatility give them the edge in this coin-flip matchup.
Wisconsin's half-court execution and rebounding should control tempo against James Madison's pace.
Duke's talent advantage is significant and their perimeter defense matches up well against Vermont's shooters.
NC State's momentum and DJ Burns' interior presence create matchup problems for Texas Tech's undersized frontcourt.
Kentucky's athleticism and depth should wear down Oakland despite the Golden Grizzlies' shooting.
Colorado's experienced backcourt and efficient half-court offense give them a slight edge over Florida.
Marquette's balanced attack and Shaka Smart's coaching give them a clear advantage at every position.
UConn is the most complete team in the field. Stetson has no matchup advantages anywhere.
Northwestern's defensive discipline and Boo Buie's shot creation give them the edge in a grind-it-out game.
San Diego State's elite defense and tournament experience from last year's Final Four run give them the edge.
Auburn's athleticism and transition game are too much for Yale to match despite the Bulldogs' shooting.
BYU's size and shooting should control the game against Duquesne's smaller lineup.
Terrence Shannon Jr. and Illinois' depth create an overwhelming talent mismatch for Morehead State.
Drake's disciplined half-court offense and strong interior defense neutralize Washington State's transition game.
Iowa State's suffocating defense and Big 12 battle-tested roster are too much for South Dakota State.
Zach Edey is an unstoppable force inside. Grambling has no answer for his size and skill.
TCU's physicality and Big 12 experience give them a slight advantage in this evenly matched game.
Gonzaga's offensive firepower and tournament pedigree make them a tough out despite McNeese's hot shooting.
Kansas' size and Bill Self's tournament preparation create too many problems for Samford's offense.
Oregon's guard play and pace create matchup problems for South Carolina's slower tempo.
Creighton's elite shooting and perimeter talent overwhelm Akron's mid-major defense.
Texas' defensive length and athleticism should contain Colorado State's efficient offense.
Tennessee's stifling defense and interior size are a nightmare matchup for Saint Peter's.
North Carolina's talent, size, and experience make this a mismatch at every position.
Tom Izzo's tournament track record and Michigan State's defensive toughness tilt this toss-up.
Saint Mary's methodical offensive system and rebounding should grind down Grand Canyon.
Alabama's explosive athleticism and three-point shooting volume overwhelm Charleston's zone.
Clemson's defensive intensity and PJ Hall's interior presence give them the edge over New Mexico.
Baylor's physicality and depth create a significant talent gap that Colgate can't overcome.
Dayton's balanced scoring attack and DaRon Holmes' two-way play should edge out Nevada.
Arizona's length and offensive versatility are too much for Long Beach State at every position.

round_of_32.reasoning: Houston's defensive intensity smothers Texas A&M. The Cougars control tempo and the glass.
Duke's talent wins out as their perimeter creation exploits Wisconsin's deliberate pace.
Kentucky's athleticism and depth should wear down NC State in the second half.
Marquette's guard depth and transition game overwhelm Colorado's half-court approach.
UConn's size and offensive versatility dismantle Northwestern's pack-line defense.

Illinois' athleticism and Terrence Shannon's shot creation overwhelm BYU's slower guards.
Iowa State's pressure defense and rebounding dominate Drake's methodical offense.
Zach Edey is simply unguardable for TCU. Purdue's size advantage is decisive.
Gonzaga's offensive execution and shooting give them a slight edge in a high-level matchup.
Creighton's shooting and offensive spacing exploit Oregon's aggressive defensive rotations.
Tennessee's elite defense suffocates Texas' inconsistent offense. The Vols control the paint.
North Carolina's offensive firepower and Armando Bacot's presence overpower Michigan State.
Alabama's pace and three-point volume disrupt Saint Mary's slow, methodical approach.
Clemson's defensive toughness and PJ Hall neutralize Baylor's frontcourt in a grind-it-out game.
Arizona's length and offensive talent overwhelm Dayton in the second half.

sweet_16.reasoning: Houston's defensive intensity smothers Duke's perimeter players and controls the boards.
Marquette's guard play and team chemistry give them the edge over Kentucky's inconsistent rotation.
UConn's offensive firepower breaks through San Diego State's defense. Too many weapons.
Illinois' offensive talent breaks Iowa State's press. Shannon and Grandison create too many scoring opportunities.
Zach Edey dominates inside and Purdue's size wears Gonzaga down in the paint.
Tennessee's defense clamps Creighton's shooters and forces tough two-point attempts.
North Carolina's rebounding and interior presence exploit Alabama's defensive weaknesses.
Arizona's offensive versatility and transition game overwhelm Clemson's half-court defense.

elite_8.reasoning: Houston's elite defense shuts down Marquette's guards. The Cougars impose their will physically.
UConn's depth and balanced scoring create too many problems for Illinois to solve defensively.
Edey's dominance inside is the difference. Tennessee's defense bends against Purdue's size.

final_four.reasoning: UConn's complete roster and defensive versatility contain North Carolina's interior attack.
Houston's perimeter defense and rebounding neutralize Purdue's size advantage.

championship.reasoning: UConn's balanced offense and depth wear down Houston in the title game. The Huskies' versatility is the difference.
